title: Glycosmis longipes
intro: Glycosmis longipes, also known as the long-stemmed Glycosmis, is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Glycosmis longipes include glycosides, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Glycosmis longipes is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
